name: FR D'Entrecasteaux
events: This convoy departed Port Said on 28 July 1944, around 1430 B/28.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was made up of the following merchant vessels; Algorab (Dutch, 4938 GRT, built 1921), Clan MacNeil (British, 6111 GRT, built 1922), Edward Bruce (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Empire Lord (British, 7359 GRT, built 1944), Fort Prudhomme (British, 7167 GRT, built 1943), Gulf of Mexico (American (tanker), 7807 GRT, built 1917), Katy (Norwegian (tanker), 6825 GRT, built 1931), Marsdale (British, 4890 GRT, built 1940), Megna (British, 6595 GRT, built 1944), Nurani (British, 5414 GRT, built 1941), Phrontis (Dutch, 6616 GRT, built 1926), Rookley (British, 4998 GRT, built 1940), Samdak (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samingoy (British, 7255 GRT, built 1944), Samtruth (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Sarpedon (British, 11321 GRT, built 1923), Suderholm (Norwegian (tanker), 4908 GRT, built 1917) and Thorshavn (Norwegian (tanker), 6869 GRT, built 1930).

The French sloop D'Entrecasteaux (?) also took passage in this convoy. She was not part of the escort though as this ship had been sunk at Diego Suarez in 1942 and had been salvaged in 1943 and made fit to proceed under her own power. She was to proceed to Bizerte where she was placed in reserve. She was never fully repaired.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was escorted by the sloop HMS Pheasant (Cdr. W.E.J. Eames, DSC, RN), the minesweeping sloops Commandant Bory (?), La Moqueuse (?) and the corvette HMS Saxifage (Lt. D.K.H. Kinloch, RNR).

On 29 July 1944, around 1600 B/29, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Alexandria; Bantria (British, 2407 GRT, built 1928), Beaconsfield (British, 4635 GRT, built 1938), City of Dundee (British, 5273 GRT, built 1921), Fort Astoria (British, 7189 GRT, built 1943), Manchester Division (British, 6048 GRT, built 1918), Samfeugh (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and Woodlark (British, 1501 GRT, built 1928). They were escorted by the sloop HMS Bideford (T/A/Lt.Cdr. H.J. Beverley, DSO, DSC, RNR) and the corvettes HMS Alisma (Lt. G. Lanning, RANVR) and HMS Spiraea (Lt. A.H. Pierce, OBE, RNR) which also joined the convoy.

At 0438 B/3, the following ships parted company with the convoy to proceed to Augusta; Algorab, Bantria, Beaconsfield, Empire Lord, Fort Astoria, Gulf of Mexico, Phrontis, Samdak, Samfeugh, Samingoy, Samtruth and Thorshavn. They were escorted to Augusta by Commandant Bory and La Moqueuse which did not rejoin the convoy.

At 0553 B/3, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Arizonan (American, 5816 GRT, built 1920), Baron Maclay (British, 6317 GRT, built 1924), Cartago (American, 4732 GRT, built 1908), Empire Airman (British (tanker), 9813 GRT, built 1942), Empire Gain (British (tanker), 3738 GRT, built 1943), Empire Spey (British, 4292 GRT, built 1929), John Sergeant (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Meline (Norwegian (tanker), 6983 GRT, built 1918), Samavon (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sheaf Crown (British, 4868 GRT, built 1929) and Toltec (Honduran, 5527 GRT, built 1929).
At 1110 B/3, the Woodlark parted company with the convoy to proceed to Malta.

At 1600 B/4, the Empire Gain, Empire Spey and Suderholm were detached to Bizerta as was the D'Entrecasteaux. The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bizerta one hour later; Chloris (British, 1171 GRT, built 1910) and Dallington Court (British, 6889 GRT, built 1929).

At 0940 B/5, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bone; Antonia (Dutch (tanker), 3357 GRT, built 1938), Clydebank (British, 5156 GRT, built 1925) and Empire Planet (British, 4290 GRT, built 1923).

At 1138 B/6, the following merchant vessels were detached to Algiers; Antonia, Arizonan, Baron Maclay, Chloris, Clydebank, Dallington Court, Empire Airman and Samavon while the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Algiers at 1300 B/6; Astrid (Norwegian, 2861 GRT, built 1942), Henry Baldwin (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), James Hoban (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Orpheus (American, 8258 GRT, built 1944), Pierre Soule (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Ponce de Leon (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Smith Thompson (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942) and William H. Wilmer (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943). Also the submarine HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) joined the convoy coming from Algiers.

At 1350 B/7, the following merchant vessels were detached to Oran; Cartago, Henry Baldwin, James Hoban, John Sergeant, Katy, Orpheus, Ponce de Leon, Smith Thompson, Toltec and William H. Wilmer while the merchant vessel Atlantic (British, 5414 GRT, built 1939) joined the convoy coming from Oran at 1445 B/7.
At 1839 B/8, the following ships were detached to Gibraltar; Astrid, Atlantic, Empire Planet, Megna, Meline and Rookley. Also detached to Gibraltar was HMS Ultimatum.
At 1916 B/8, the Mediterranean escort, HMS Pheasant, HMS Bideford, HMS Alisma, HMS Saxifrage and HMS Spiraea was detached to Gibraltar.

This was after a new escort had joined up with the remaining ships of the convoy which were now proceeding into the Atlantic together with joiners from Gibraltar. The convoy was now known as convoy 'MKS 57(G)'. This convoy was to make rendezvous with and join convoy SL 166 coming from Freetown. These two convoy were then to proceed in company, as the combined convoy SL 166 / MKS 57(G), to the U.K. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
